title: Least Common Multiple
description: Given two positive integers a and b, calculate the least common multiple (LCM) of these two numbers. The LCM of a and b is the smallest positive integer that is divisible by both a and b.

examples.explanation: The multiples of 4 are {4, 8, 12, 16, ...} and the multiples of 6 are {6, 12, 18, 24, ...}. The smallest common multiple is 12.
The multiples of 15 are {15, 30, 45, ...} and the multiples of 10 are {10, 20, 30, ...}. The smallest common multiple is 30.
The multiples of 7 are {7, 14, 21, 28, 35, ...} and the multiples of 5 are {5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, ...}. The smallest common multiple is 35.

constraints: The solution should be computed efficiently.
hints: Consider using the relationship between GCD and LCM.
The formula for LCM can be defined as: LCM(a, b) = (a * b) / GCD(a, b).
Think about how to compute the GCD efficiently.
You may want to handle large products carefully to avoid overflow.
